behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction specifies exact variable names (org_id, user_auth, api_key_org_id), method names (get_api_key_org_id), and attribute (user_auth, current_org_id) that tests check.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction dictates the exact code patterns verbatim: 'if hasattr(self.user_context, \'user_auth\'):', 'api_key_org_id = user_auth.get_api_key_org_id()', 'if api_key_org_id is not None:', 'org_id = api_key_org_id' — this is the solution written out.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check behavior (metadata.org_id equality) via mocks, not exact variable names or line positions, so alternative correct fixes would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests import SaasSQLAppConversationInfoService and call save_app_conversation_info, then inspect mock_db_session.add calls.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests assert metadata.org_id matches specific UUIDs based on auth scenario; a stub wouldn't produce the correct org_id across all three cases.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three scenarios (API key with org, legacy API key, cookie auth) described in instruction are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have clear docstrings, fixtures, and section headers like '===== Fail-to-Pass Tests ====='.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction prescribes the exact code lines to write (e.g., 'assign exactly: org_id = api_key_org_id', 'Check for the org ID method using: if hasattr(user_auth, \'get_api_key_org_id\'):'), effectively dictating the fix.
